scenarios.opening_query: My e-bike's brakes gave out on a hill and I went over the bars into a parked car.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The brake model was recalled two months before my crash for exactly this failure — the shop never notified me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: That brake model was recalled two months before my crash — the shop never told me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a recalled component caused the crash
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The brakes were fine — a driver flung his door open into the bike lane and I had nowhere to go.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Brakes were fine — a driver doored me in the bike lane.

scenarios.opening_query: The haunted house attraction hurt my back when a floor panel dropped further than it should have.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the county fairgrounds' own Halloween event, run by parks staff — public entity, notice deadline running.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the county fairgrounds' own event with parks staff — a public entity, and the notice clock is running.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the attraction operator is a public entity
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private company that rents the warehouse every October — their waiver and insurance are the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A private company renting the warehouse — their waiver and insurer are the fight.
scenarios.vetting_note: Operator identity in a seasonal-attraction setting.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter's cheer team practices on mats the gym knows are worn out, and she finally got hurt.
scenarios.final: Administrative Law > Schools — Special Needs Education
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || Administrative Law > Schools — Special Needs Education
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's her school's team and she has a 504 plan the coach ignored — cleared only for limited impact after her concussion, yet he ran her on full tumbling passes; the school's plan violation is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's her school team — she has a 504 plan limiting impact after her concussion, and the coach ran full tumbling anyway. The plan violation is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a disability-plan violation at a school underlies the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private club gym, no school or plan involved — the worn equipment they'd been warned about is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a private club gym — the worn mats they were warned about are the case.
scenarios.vetting_note: 504-plan violation reroutes to education law; club-gym version stays premises.
